message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (for a code change the named test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if reverted; for a doc/decision item, judge whether the documented claim is now accurate/honest). Repo /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix H-010 in src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py
What: stable_seed docstring corrected: truncated seed gives uniqueness only to the birthday bound, not guaranteed.
Test(s): (doc; no test)
Read the actual changed file(s); read the test; check no regression. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN. Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

011942b fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
7dccfd8 fix(hardening-p4): batch E — egress, error contracts, PII & test discrimination (8 findings)
82f58ca fix(hardening-p3): batch 5 — performance, with two scoped declines (5 findings)
1e3dce5 harden/pass-2: 33 production-readiness fixes (#124-#156) (#157)
1389242 harden/pass-1: 22 production-readiness fixes (assess→2-agent verify→fix→2-agent confirm) (#123)
f9d8cb4 feat: complete the composition layer + public-readiness (audit close-out) (#98)
3ce9605 feat(methodology): enforce pre-registration integrity by construction (ADR-0009) (#79)
bd91ceb feat(baseline): determinism harness + dependency-light primitives (#63)

1	"""Determinism & reproducibility harness for the baseline (ADR-0001, METHODOLOGY.md §1, §7).
2	
3	A baseline is only a yardstick if the *same inputs reproduce the same baseline*. This module
4	makes that contract concrete and engine-level: it content-hashes inputs, derives seeds
5	deterministically from those hashes, records component versions, and stamps all of it into a
6	:class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineArtifact`'s ``determinism`` record — so a
7	hostile reviewer can confirm a baseline was produced exactly as claimed.
8	
9	It is dependency-free and pure. The heavy, *choice-bearing* builders (which embedding model,
10	which clustering algorithm, which graph construction) encode dataset/study decisions and are
11	supplied by a study's adapter as :class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineBuilder`
12	implementations — not prescribed by the engine (ADR-0002). This harness records *how* such a
13	builder ran so the result is reproducible regardless of which builder it was.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import hashlib
19	import json
20	import sys
21	from dataclasses import dataclass
22	from typing import Any, Mapping
23	
24	from assay_engine import __version__ as _ENGINE_VERSION
25	
26	# The canonical hashing primitives moved to ``assay_engine._canonical`` (see note below); these
27	# imports re-export them at their historical home so existing call sites keep working.
28	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_plain as _plain  # noqa: F401
29	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_bytes as hash_bytes  # noqa: F401  (re-export)
30	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_text as hash_text  # noqa: F401  (re-export, historical home)
31	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_value as hash_value  # re-export (baseline public API)
32	from assay_engine._canonical import keytag as _keytag  # noqa: F401
33	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
34	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact
35	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
36	
37	# The canonical hashing primitives (``_plain``/``_keytag``/``hash_bytes``/``hash_text``/
38	# ``hash_value``) live in ``assay_engine._canonical`` so they sit below both ``baseline`` and
39	# ``methodology`` in the import graph (audit #B1/#B2/#B5 originally landed here; hoisted so
40	# pre-registration can content-bind a hypothesis with the *same* type-faithful digest). They are
41	# re-exported above for back-compat.
42	
43	
44	def corpus_fingerprint(corpus: Corpus) -> str:
45	    """Deterministic content hash of a corpus (order-independent across units/relations).
46	
47	    The fingerprint is SHA-256 over the canonical JSON of the corpus payload. The JSON is
48	    streamed into the hash via ``JSONEncoder.iterencode`` rather than materialized as one giant
49	    string (pass 3, #F-016): for a large corpus this avoids holding the full serialized payload
50	    in RAM on top of the row dicts at hash time. ``iterencode`` with the same options yields the
51	    identical byte stream ``json.dumps`` would, so the fingerprint is byte-for-byte unchanged.
52	    """
53	    units = [
54	        {"id": u.unit_id, "text": u.text, "attrs": _plain(u.attributes)}
55	        for u in sorted(corpus.units, key=lambda u: u.unit_id)
56	    ]
57	    relations = [
58	        {"s": r.source_id, "t": r.target_id, "k": r.kind, "attrs": _plain(r.attributes)}
59	        for r in sorted(corpus.relations, key=lambda r: (r.source_id, r.target_id, r.kind))
60	    ]
61	    payload = {"units": units, "relations": relations, "metadata": _plain(corpus.metadata)}
62	    h = hashlib.sha256()
63	    encoder = json.JSONEncoder(sort_keys=True, allow_nan=False)
64	    for chunk in encoder.iterencode(payload):
65	        h.update(chunk.encode("utf-8"))
66	    return h.hexdigest()
67	
68	
69	def stable_seed(*parts: str, bits: int = 32) -> int:
70	    """A deterministic non-negative integer seed derived from ``parts``.
71	
72	    Derived from a SHA-256 hash so the same inputs always yield the same seed — reproducible
73	    without a hard-coded magic number. The result is truncated to ``bits`` (default 32) to fit a
74	    typical RNG seed, so distinct inputs get distinct seeds only up to the birthday bound of that
75	    space (≈1 in 2**bits per pair) — NOT a guarantee of uniqueness (pass 5, #H-010). The full
76	    content hash (``corpus_fingerprint`` / ``hash_value``) is the collision-resistant identity;
77	    this seed is only for reproducible RNG initialization, where a rare collision is harmless.
78	    """
79	    digest = hashlib.sha256("\x1f".join(parts).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
80	    return int(digest, 16) % (1 << bits)
81	
82	
83	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
84	class DeterminismRecord:
85	    """The reproducibility provenance stamped onto a baseline (ADR-0001)."""
86	
87	    seed: int
88	    input_hashes: Mapping[str, str]
89	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str]
90	
91	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
92	        object.__setattr__(self, "input_hashes", freeze_mapping(self.input_hashes))
93	        object.__setattr__(self, "component_versions", freeze_mapping(self.component_versions))
94	
95	    def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
96	        return {
97	            "seed": self.seed,
98	            "input_hashes": dict(self.input_hashes),
99	            "component_versions": dict(self.component_versions),
100	        }
101	
102	
103	def build_baseline_artifact(
104	    corpus: Corpus,
105	    contents: Mapping[str, Any],
106	    *,
107	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
108	    extra_inputs: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
109	    seed: int | None = None,
110	    corpus_hash: str | None = None,
111	    trust_corpus_hash: bool = False,
112	) -> BaselineArtifact:
113	    """Assemble a :class:`BaselineArtifact` with a complete determinism record.
114	
115	    ``contents`` is the builder's output (embeddings, similarity graph, clusters, …).
116	    ``component_versions`` records the builder + any model/library versions (the engine and
117	    Python versions are added automatically). ``extra_inputs`` are additional inputs to hash
118	    (e.g. config). ``seed``, if omitted, is derived deterministically from the corpus + inputs
119	    so it is reproducible.
120	
121	    ``corpus_hash`` lets a caller pass an already-computed :func:`corpus_fingerprint` (#119). By
122	    default it is **verified** against the corpus and a mismatch raises ``ValueError`` — a
123	    stale/typo'd hash would otherwise produce a determinism record whose fingerprint and derived
124	    seed describe a *different* corpus than the one passed, silently defeating the reproducibility
125	    guarantee this harness exists to provide (#155). Pass ``trust_corpus_hash=True`` to skip the
126	    recompute (the #119 optimization) only when the caller computed the hash from this exact
127	    corpus and accepts integrity responsibility.
128	    """
129	    if corpus_hash is None:
130	        corpus_hash = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
131	    elif not trust_corpus_hash:
132	        # Verify the supplied hash binds the corpus actually passed (default-safe, #155).
133	        actual = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
134	        if corpus_hash != actual:
135	            raise ValueError(
136	                f"corpus_hash {corpus_hash!r} does not match the corpus "
137	                f"(corpus_fingerprint={actual!r}) — the determinism record would bind the wrong "
138	                "data; pass trust_corpus_hash=True only if you accept integrity responsibility"
139	            )
140	    if extra_inputs and "corpus" in extra_inputs:
141	        raise ValueError("'corpus' is a reserved input-hash key — rename the extra input (#B7)")
142	    input_hashes = {"corpus": corpus_hash}
143	    for name, value in (extra_inputs or {}).items():
144	        # Name the offending key when an extra input is not canonicalizable (#G-021): hash_value
145	        # raises a raw ValueError (non-finite) / TypeError (unreproducible repr) with no context,
146	        # so a rich config with one bad value gave an opaque error. Re-raise with the key.
147	        try:
148	            input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value)
149	        except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
150	            raise type(exc)(f"extra_input {name!r} is not canonicalizable: {exc}") from exc
151	
152	    versions = {"engine": _ENGINE_VERSION, "python": sys.version.split()[0]}
153	    versions.update(component_versions or {})
154	
155	    if seed is None:
156	        # Derive from all input hashes (the corpus hash is already among them under "corpus").
157	        seed = stable_seed(*(input_hashes[k] for k in sorted(input_hashes)))
158	
159	    record = DeterminismRecord(seed=seed, input_hashes=input_hashes, component_versions=versions)
160	    return BaselineArtifact(
161	        corpus_fingerprint=corpus_hash, contents=contents, determinism=record.as_dict()
162	    )
163

content.text: Let me verify the diff and confirm the original docstring was incorrect.

content.content:
commit 011942ba0d024960245c8ab919fdf86388cff686
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 01:31:14 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
    
    Pass-5 confirmed findings (2-agent verified after self-refutation; revert-discriminated):
    
    Reliability / error contracts:
    - H-004 (med) malformed reasoning params (temperature/seed) raise the typed
      PermanentReasoningError instead of a raw ValueError/TypeError leaking past run()/run_json.
    - H-021 (low) QdrantVectorStore.query rejects non-positive k and skips returned points
      lacking our 'ref' payload (was an opaque KeyError).
    - H-022 (low) vectorstore int env vars parse via _int_env, naming the bad var instead of an
      opaque import-time "invalid literal for int()".
    - H-013 (low) the provenance-trail artifact tempfile is unlinked after the tracker copies it
      (was delete=False, leaked per tracked run).
    
    CI / supply-chain:
    - H-007 (med) license gate surfaces UNKNOWN/empty-license packages as a ::warning:: (hidden-
      copyleft vector) instead of passing them silently; still fails only on explicit deny matches.
    - H-008 (med) workflow pins GITHUB_TOKEN to least-privilege `permissions: contents: read`.
    - H-009 (med) the integration job verifies the Postgres/Qdrant service containers are
      reachable (pg_isready + /readyz) so the live tests can't silently SKIP and pass vacuously.
    
    Observability / governance:
    - H-011 (med) documented the deliberate recorder-optionality asymmetry between the graph gate
      node and run_study (the runner owns + always records to the trail; the node bridges).
    - H-017 (low) documented that serialized provenance records don't embed keying — the verifier
      supplies the secret out-of-band (key management; verify_records fails closed).
    
    Documentation honesty (assertion-vs-implementation):
    - H-010 (med) stable_seed: "distinct inputs get distinct seeds" corrected to the birthday
      bound of the truncated space (not a uniqueness guarantee).
    - H-012 (low) gatenode "atomically"/"terminal idempotency" claims scoped honestly.
    - H-023 (low) redact_creds "always scrubbed precisely" corrected to "precise when the conn
      string appears verbatim" (reformatted strings fall to the regex backstops).
    - H-018 (med) transcript scrubber redacts the username in the project-dir slug at any length.
    - H-024 (low) the in-process unfreeze test is marked illustrative; the seed-pinned subprocess
      test is the discriminating guard.
    
    Split-verdict (1-of-2) findings H-005/H-006/H-019/H-020 and rejected H-014 are documented in
    PASS-5.md as not-proceeding per the both-must-confirm protocol.
    
    Gates: ruff + ruff format --check + mypy --strict clean; 531 passed, 5 skipped.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

diff --git a/src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py b/src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py
index 124bd7d..2a1f2e8 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py
@@ -69,8 +69,12 @@ def corpus_fingerprint(corpus: Corpus) -> str:
 def stable_seed(*parts: str, bits: int = 32) -> int:
     """A deterministic non-negative integer seed derived from ``parts``.
 
-    Derived from a hash so the same inputs always yield the same seed — reproducible without a
-    hard-coded magic number, and distinct inputs get distinct seeds.
+    Derived from a SHA-256 hash so the same inputs always yield the same seed — reproducible
+    without a hard-coded magic number. The result is truncated to ``bits`` (default 32) to fit a
+    typical RNG seed, so distinct inputs get distinct seeds only up to the birthday bound of that
+    space (≈1 in 2**bits per pair) — NOT a guarantee of uniqueness (pass 5, #H-010). The full
+    content hash (``corpus_fingerprint`` / ``hash_value``) is the collision-resistant identity;
+    this seed is only for reproducible RNG initialization, where a rare collision is harmless.
     """
     digest = hashlib.sha256("\x1f".join(parts).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
     return int(digest, 16) % (1 << bits)

content.text:
The fix is a documentation-only change. Let me verify the technical accuracy of the corrected claim against the implementation.

The implementation: `int(digest, 16) % (1 << bits)` — takes SHA-256, reduces mod 2^bits. With a 32-bit space (default), distinct inputs producing distinct seeds is genuinely NOT guaranteed — the modular reduction maps the 256-bit digest into 2^32 buckets, so collisions are possible. The original docstring claimed "distinct inputs get distinct seeds" which is false.

The corrected claim:
- "truncated to bits (default 32)" — accurate (mod 2^bits)
- "distinct inputs get distinct seeds only up to the birthday bound" — accurate; collisions become likely around sqrt(2^bits) = 2^16 inputs
- "≈1 in 2**bits per pair" — accurate; for any specific pair of distinct inputs, collision probability ≈ 1/2^bits (assuming SHA-256 behaves as a uniform map)
- The pointer to corpus_fingerprint/hash_value as the collision-resistant identity is accurate, and the rationale (seed only for RNG init, rare collision harmless) is sound.

The original wording said "Derived from a hash" — now correctly specified as SHA-256, matching `hashlib.sha256`. No regression: pure docstring, no behavior change. The fix references #H-010 properly.

This is a doc item with no test (correctly stated as "doc; no test"). The judgment criterion is whether the documented claim is now accurate/honest — it is. The previous claim was a genuine overstatement; the correction is technically precise.

input.reasoning: Doc-only fix in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py (stable_seed, commit 011942b). APPROPRIATE: pure docstring change, no behavior change, no regression. The original claim "distinct inputs get distinct seeds" was genuinely false: the implementation is `int(sha256_digest, 16) % (1 << bits)` (default bits=32), which folds a 256-bit digest into a 2^32 space, so collisions are possible — uniqueness is NOT guaranteed. The corrected text is technically accurate on every point: (a) "truncated to bits (default 32)" matches the mod 2^bits operation; (b) "distinct seeds only up to the birthday bound" is correct (collisions become likely near 2^(bits/2)); (c) "≈1 in 2**bits per pair" is the correct per-pair collision probability for a near-uniform map; (d) it also correctly upgrades "a hash" to "SHA-256", matching hashlib.sha256; (e) the pointer that corpus_fingerprint/hash_value (full digest) is the collision-resistant identity while this seed is only for reproducible RNG init is sound and consistent with how stable_seed is actually used in build_baseline_artifact. COMPLETE: this is a documentation/honesty item correctly marked "doc; no test" — the criterion is whether the documented claim is now accurate, and it is. The previous statement was an overstatement; the correction is honest and precise.

content.text: Verified. CONFIRMED — the H-010 fix is both appropriate and complete.
